text: Let him kiss<S>5401</S> me with the kisses<S>5390</S> of his mouth<S>6310</S>; For thy love<S>1730</S> is better<S>2896</S> than wine<S>3196</S>.
Thine oils<S>8081</S> have a goodly<S>2896</S> fragrance<S>7381</S>; Thy name<S>8034</S> is as oil<S>8081</S> poured forth<S>7324</S>; Therefore do the virgins<S>5959</S> love thee<S>157</S>.

Draw<S>4900</S> me<S>7323</S>; we will run after<S>310</S> thee<S>4428</S>: The king hath brought<S>935</S> me into his chambers<S>2315</S>; We will be glad<S>1523</S> and rejoice<S>8055</S> in thee<S>2142</S>; We will make mention of thy love<S>1730</S> more than of wine<S>3196</S>: Rightly<S>4339</S> do they love thee<S>157</S>.
I am black<S>7838</S>, but comely<S>5000</S>, Oh ye daughters<S>1323</S> of Jerusalem<S>3389</S>, As the tents<S>168</S> of Kedar<S>6938</S>, As the curtains<S>3407</S> of Solomon<S>8010</S>.

Look<S>7200</S> not upon me<S>7840</S>, because I am swarthy, Because the sun<S>8121</S> hath scorched<S>7805</S> me<S>517</S>. My mother's sons<S>1121</S> were incensed<S>2787</S> against me<S>7760</S>; They made me keeper<S>5201</S> of the vineyards<S>3754</S>; But mine own vineyard<S>3754</S> have I not kept<S>5201</S>.

Tell<S>5046</S> me<S>5315</S>, O thou whom my soul loveth<S>157</S>, Where<S>349</S> thou feedest<S>7462</S> thy flock<S>349</S>, Where thou makest it to rest<S>7257</S> at noon<S>6672</S>: For<S>4100</S> why should I be as one that is veiled Beside the flocks<S>5739</S> of thy companions<S>2270</S>?

If thou know<S>3045</S> not<S>3303</S>, O thou fairest among women<S>802</S>, Go thy way forth<S>3318</S> by the footsteps<S>6119</S> of the flock<S>6629</S>, And feed<S>7462</S> thy kids<S>1429</S> beside the shepherds<S>7462</S>' tents<S>4908</S>.
I have compared<S>1819</S> thee<S>7474</S>, O my love, To a steed<S>5484</S> in Pharaoh<S>6547</S>'s chariots<S>7393</S>.
Thy cheeks<S>3895</S> are comely<S>4998</S> with plaits<S>8447</S> of hair<S>6677</S>, Thy neck with strings of jewels<S>2737</S>.
We will make<S>6213</S> thee plaits<S>8447</S> of gold<S>2091</S> With studs<S>5351</S> of silver<S>3701</S>.
While the king<S>4428</S> sat at his table<S>4524</S>, My spikenard<S>5373</S> sent forth<S>5414</S> its fragrance<S>7381</S>.
My beloved<S>1730</S> is unto me as a bundle<S>6872</S> of myrrh<S>4753</S>, That lieth<S>3885</S> betwixt my breasts<S>7699</S>.
My beloved<S>1730</S> is unto me as a cluster<S>811</S> of henna-flowers<S>3724</S> In the vineyards<S>3754</S> of En-gedi<S>5872</S>.
Behold<S>3303</S>, thou art fair, my love<S>7474</S>; Behold thou art fair<S>3303</S>; Thine eyes<S>5869</S> are as doves<S>3123</S>.
Behold<S>3303</S>, thou art fair, my beloved<S>1730</S>, yea<S>5273</S>, pleasant: Also our couch<S>6210</S> is green<S>7488</S>.
The beams<S>6982</S> of our house<S>1004</S> are cedars<S>730</S>, And our rafters<S>7351</S> are firs<S>1266</S>.
I am a rose<S>2261</S> of Sharon<S>8289</S>, A lily<S>7799</S> of the valleys<S>6010</S>.
As a lily<S>7799</S> among thorns<S>2336</S>, So is my love<S>7474</S> among the daughters<S>1323</S>.

As the apple-tree<S>8598</S> among the trees<S>8598</S> of the wood<S>8598</S>, So is my beloved<S>1730</S> among the sons<S>1121</S>. I sat<S>3427</S> down under his shadow<S>6738</S> with great delight<S>2530</S>, And his fruit<S>6529</S> was sweet<S>4966</S> to my taste<S>2441</S>.
He brought<S>935</S> me to the banqueting<S>3196</S> - house<S>1004</S>, And his banner<S>1714</S> over me was love<S>160</S>.
Stay<S>5564</S> ye me with raisins<S>809</S>, refresh<S>7502</S> me with apples<S>8598</S>; For I am sick<S>2470</S> from love<S>160</S>.
His left hand<S>8040</S> is under my head<S>7218</S>, And his right hand<S>3225</S> doth embrace me<S>2263</S>.

I adjure<S>7650</S> you<S>1323</S>, O daughters of Jerusalem<S>3389</S>, By the roes<S>6643</S>, or<S>176</S> by the hinds<S>355</S> of the field<S>7704</S>, That ye stir not up<S>5782</S>, nor awake<S>5782</S> my love<S>160</S>, Until he please<S>2654</S>.
The voice<S>6963</S> of my beloved<S>1730</S>! behold<S>935</S>, he cometh, Leaping<S>1801</S> upon the mountains<S>2022</S>, Skipping<S>7092</S> upon the hills<S>1389</S>.

My beloved<S>1730</S> is like<S>1819</S> a roe<S>6643</S> or a young<S>6082</S> hart<S>354</S>: Behold<S>5975</S>, he standeth behind<S>310</S> our wall<S>3796</S>; He looketh<S>7688</S> in at the windows<S>2474</S>; He glanceth<S>6692</S> through the lattice<S>2762</S>.
My beloved<S>1730</S> spake<S>6030</S>, and said<S>559</S> unto me<S>6965</S>, Rise up, my love<S>7474</S>, my fair one<S>3303</S>, and come away<S>3212</S>.
For<S>5638</S>, lo, the winter is past<S>5674</S>; The rain<S>1653</S> is over<S>2498</S> and gone<S>1980</S>;

The flowers<S>5339</S> appear<S>7200</S> on the earth<S>776</S>; The time<S>6256</S> of the singing<S>2158</S> of birds is come<S>5060</S>, And the voice<S>6963</S> of the turtle-dove<S>8449</S> is heard<S>8085</S> in our land<S>776</S>;

The fig-tree<S>8384</S> ripeneth<S>2590</S> her green figs<S>6291</S>, And the vines<S>1612</S> are in blossom<S>5563</S>; They give<S>5414</S> forth their fragrance<S>7381</S>. Arise<S>6965</S>, my love<S>7474</S>, my fair one<S>3303</S>, and come away<S>3212</S>.

O my dove<S>3123</S>, that art in the clefts<S>2288</S> of the rock<S>5553</S>, In the covert<S>5643</S> of the steep place<S>4095</S>, Let me see<S>7200</S> thy countenance<S>4758</S>, Let me hear<S>8085</S> thy voice<S>6963</S>; For sweet<S>6156</S> is thy voice<S>6963</S>, and thy countenance<S>4758</S> is comely<S>5000</S>.
Take<S>270</S> us the foxes<S>7776</S>, the little<S>6996</S> foxes<S>7776</S>, That spoil<S>2254</S> the vineyards<S>3754</S>; For our vineyards<S>3754</S> are in blossom<S>5563</S>.
My beloved<S>1730</S> is mine<S>7462</S>, and I am his: He feedeth his flock among the lilies<S>7799</S>.

Until the day<S>3117</S> be cool<S>6315</S>, and the shadows<S>6752</S> flee away<S>5127</S>, Turn<S>5437</S>, my beloved<S>1730</S>, and be thou like<S>1819</S> a roe<S>6643</S> or a young<S>6082</S> hart<S>354</S> Upon the mountains<S>2022</S> of Bether<S>1336</S>.
By night<S>3915</S> on my bed<S>4904</S> I sought<S>1245</S> him whom my soul<S>5315</S> loveth<S>157</S>: I sought<S>1245</S> him<S>4672</S>, but I found him not.

I said<S>6965</S>, I will rise now<S>5437</S>, and go about the city<S>5892</S>; In the streets<S>7784</S> and in the broad ways<S>7339</S> I will seek<S>1245</S> him whom my soul<S>5315</S> loveth<S>157</S>: I sought<S>1245</S> him<S>4672</S>, but I found him not.
The watchmen<S>8104</S> that go about<S>5437</S> the city<S>5892</S> found<S>4672</S> me<S>7200</S>; To whom I said, Saw ye him whom my soul<S>5315</S> loveth<S>157</S>?

It was but a little<S>4592</S> that I passed<S>5674</S> from them<S>4672</S>, When I found him whom my soul<S>5315</S> loveth<S>157</S>: I held<S>270</S> him<S>7503</S>, and would not let him go, Until I had brought<S>935</S> him into my mother<S>517</S>'s house<S>1004</S>, And into the chamber<S>2315</S> of her that conceived me<S>2029</S>.

I adjure<S>7650</S> you<S>1323</S>, O daughters of Jerusalem<S>3389</S>, By the roes<S>6643</S>, or<S>176</S> by the hinds<S>355</S> of the field<S>7704</S>, That ye stir not up<S>5782</S>, nor awake<S>5782</S> my love<S>160</S>, Until he please<S>2654</S>.

Who is this that cometh<S>5927</S> up from the wilderness<S>4057</S> Like pillars<S>8490</S> of smoke<S>6227</S>, Perfumed<S>6999</S> with myrrh<S>4753</S> and frankincense<S>3828</S>, With all powders<S>81</S> of the merchant<S>7402</S>?
Behold<S>8010</S>, it is the litter<S>4296</S> Threescore<S>8346</S> mighty<S>1368</S> men are about<S>5439</S> it<S>1368</S>, Of the mighty men of Israel<S>3478</S>.

They all handle<S>270</S> the sword<S>2719</S>, and are expert<S>3925</S> in war<S>4421</S>: Every man<S>376</S> hath his sword<S>2719</S> upon his thigh<S>3409</S>, Because of fear<S>6343</S> in the night<S>3915</S>.
King<S>4428</S> Solomon<S>8010</S> made<S>6213</S> himself a palanquin<S>668</S> Of the wood<S>6086</S> of Lebanon<S>3844</S>.

He made<S>6213</S> the pillars<S>5982</S> thereof of silver<S>3701</S>, The bottom<S>7507</S> thereof of gold<S>2091</S>, the seat<S>4817</S> of it of purple<S>713</S>, The midst<S>8432</S> thereof being paved<S>7528</S> with love<S>160</S>, From the daughters<S>1323</S> of Jerusalem<S>3389</S>.

Go forth<S>3318</S>, O ye daughters<S>1323</S> of Zion<S>6726</S>, and behold<S>7200</S> king<S>4428</S> Solomon<S>8010</S>, With the crown<S>5850</S> wherewith his mother<S>517</S> hath crowned<S>5849</S> him In the day<S>3117</S> of his espousals<S>2861</S>, And in the day<S>3117</S> of the gladness<S>8057</S> of his heart<S>3820</S>.

Behold<S>3303</S>, thou art fair, my love<S>7474</S>; behold<S>3303</S>, thou art fair; Thine eyes<S>5869</S> are as doves<S>3123</S> behind<S>1157</S> thy veil<S>6777</S>. Thy hair<S>8181</S> is as a flock<S>5739</S> of goats<S>5795</S>, That lie<S>1570</S> along the side of mount<S>2022</S> Gilead<S>1568</S>.

Thy teeth<S>8127</S> are like a flock<S>5739</S> of ewes that are newly shorn<S>7094</S>, Which are come up<S>5927</S> from the washing<S>7367</S>, Whereof every one hath twins<S>8382</S>, And none is bereaved among them<S>7909</S>.

Thy lips<S>8193</S> are like a thread<S>2339</S> of scarlet<S>8144</S>, And thy mouth<S>4057</S> is comely<S>5000</S>. Thy temples<S>7541</S> are like a piece<S>6400</S> of a pomegranate<S>7416</S> behind<S>1157</S> thy veil<S>6777</S>.

Thy neck<S>6677</S> is like the tower<S>4026</S> of David<S>1732</S> builded<S>1129</S> for an armory<S>8530</S>, Whereon there hang<S>8518</S> a thousand<S>505</S> bucklers<S>4043</S>, All the shields<S>7982</S> of the mighty men<S>1368</S>.
Thy two<S>8147</S> breasts<S>7699</S> are like two<S>8147, Joshua82</S> fawns<S>6646</S> That are twins<S>8380</S> of a roe<S>7462</S>, Which feed among the lilies<S>7799</S>.

Until the day<S>3117</S> be cool<S>6315</S>, and the shadows<S>6752</S> flee away<S>5127</S>, I will get<S>3212</S> me to the mountain<S>2022</S> of myrrh<S>4753</S>, And to the hill<S>1389</S> of frankincense<S>3828</S>.
Thou art all fair<S>3303</S>, my love<S>7474</S>; And there is no spot in thee<S>3971</S>.

Come<S>935</S> with me from Lebanon<S>3844</S>, my bride<S>3618</S>, With me from Lebanon<S>3844</S>: Look<S>7789</S> from the top<S>7218</S> of Amana<S>549</S>, From the top<S>7218</S> of Senir<S>8149</S> and Hermon<S>2768</S>, From the lions<S>738</S>' dens<S>4585</S>, From the mountains<S>2042</S> of the leopards<S>5246</S>.

Thou hast ravished my heart<S>3823</S>, my sister<S>269</S>, my bride<S>3618</S>; Thou hast ravished my heart<S>3823</S> with one<S>259</S> of thine eyes<S>5869</S>, With one<S>259</S> chain<S>6060</S> of thy neck<S>6677</S>.

How fair<S>3302</S> is thy love<S>1730</S>, my sister<S>269</S>, my bride<S>3618</S>! How much better<S>2895</S> is thy love<S>1730</S> than wine<S>3196</S>! And the fragrance<S>7381</S> of thine oils<S>8081</S> than all manner of spices<S>1314</S>!

Thy lips<S>8193</S>, O my bride<S>3618</S>, drop<S>5197</S> as the honeycomb<S>5317</S>: Honey<S>1706</S> and milk<S>2461</S> are under thy tongue<S>3956</S>; And the smell<S>7381</S> of thy garments<S>8008</S> is like the smell<S>7381</S> of Lebanon<S>3844</S>.
A garden<S>1588</S> shut<S>5274</S> up is my sister<S>269</S>, my bride<S>3618</S>; A spring<S>1530</S> shut up<S>5274</S>, a fountain<S>4599</S> sealed<S>2856</S>.
Thy shoots<S>7973</S> are an orchard<S>6508</S> of pomegranates<S>7416</S>, with precious<S>4022</S> fruits<S>6529</S>; Henna<S>3724</S> with spikenard plants<S>5373</S>,

Spikenard<S>5373</S> and saffron<S>3750</S>, Calamus<S>7070</S> and cinnamon<S>7076</S>, with all trees<S>6086</S> of frankincense<S>3828</S>; Myrrh<S>4753</S> and aloes<S>174</S>, with all the chief<S>7218</S> spices<S>1314</S>.
Thou art a fountain<S>4599</S> of gardens<S>1588</S>, A well<S>875</S> of living<S>2416</S> waters<S>4325</S>, And flowing streams<S>5140</S> from Lebanon<S>3844</S>.

Awake<S>5782</S>, O north wind<S>6828</S>; And come<S>935</S>, thou south<S>8486</S>; Blow<S>6315</S> upon my garden<S>1588</S>, that the spices<S>1314</S> thereof may flow out<S>5140</S>. Let my beloved<S>1730</S> come<S>935</S> into his garden<S>1588</S>, And eat<S>398</S> his precious<S>4022</S> fruits<S>6529</S>.

I am come<S>935</S> into my garden<S>1588</S>, my sister<S>269</S>, my bride<S>3618</S>: I have gathered<S>717</S> my myrrh<S>4753</S> with my spice<S>1313</S>; I have eaten<S>398</S> my honeycomb<S>3293</S> with my honey<S>1706</S>; I have drunk<S>8354</S> my wine<S>3196</S> with my milk<S>2461</S>. Eat<S>398</S>, O friends<S>7453</S>; Drink<S>8354</S>, yea<S>7937</S>, drink abundantly, O beloved<S>1730</S>.

I was asleep<S>3463</S>, but my heart<S>3820</S> waked<S>5782</S>: It is the voice<S>6963</S> of my beloved<S>1730</S> that knocketh<S>1849</S>, saying<S>6605</S>, Open to me<S>269</S>, my sister, my love<S>7474</S>, my dove<S>3123</S>, my undefiled<S>8535</S>; For my head<S>7218</S> is filled<S>4390</S> with dew<S>2919</S>, My locks<S>6977</S> with the drops<S>7447</S> of the night<S>3915</S>.
I have put off<S>6584</S> my garment<S>3801</S>; How<S>349</S> shall I put it on<S>3847</S>? I have washed<S>7364</S> my feet<S>7272</S>; how<S>349</S> shall I defile them<S>2936</S>?
My beloved<S>1730</S> put in<S>7971</S> his hand<S>3027</S> by<S>4480</S> the hole<S>2356</S> of the door<S>4578</S>, And my heart was moved for him<S>1993</S>.

I rose up<S>6965</S> to open<S>6605</S> to my beloved<S>1730</S>; And my hands<S>3027</S> dropped<S>5197</S> with myrrh<S>4753</S>, And my fingers<S>676</S> with liquid<S>5674</S> myrrh<S>4753</S>, Upon the handles<S>3709</S> of the bolt<S>4514</S>.

I opened<S>6605</S> to my beloved<S>1730</S>; But my beloved<S>1730</S> had withdrawn<S>2559</S> himself<S>5674</S>, and was gone. My soul<S>5315</S> had failed<S>3318</S> me when he spake<S>1696</S>: I sought<S>1245</S> him<S>4672</S>, but I could not find him<S>7121</S>; I called him<S>6030</S>, but he gave me no answer.

The watchmen<S>8104</S> that go about<S>5437</S> the city<S>5892</S> found<S>4672</S> me<S>5221</S>, They smote me<S>6481</S>, they wounded me<S>8104</S>; The keepers of the walls<S>2346</S> took away<S>5375</S> my mantle from me<S>7289</S>.
I adjure<S>7650</S> you<S>1323</S>, O daughters of Jerusalem<S>3389</S>, If ye find<S>4672</S> my beloved<S>1730</S>, That ye tell<S>5046</S> him<S>2470</S>, that I am sick from love<S>160</S>.

What is thy beloved<S>1730</S> more than another beloved<S>1730</S>, O thou fairest<S>3303</S> among women<S>802</S>? What is thy beloved<S>1730</S> more than another beloved<S>1730</S>, That thou<S>3602</S> dost so adjure us<S>7650</S>?
My beloved<S>1730</S> is white<S>6703</S> and ruddy<S>122</S>, The chiefest<S>1713</S> among ten thousand<S>7233</S>.
His head<S>7218</S> is as the most<S>3800</S> fine gold<S>6337</S>; His locks<S>6977</S> are bushy<S>8534</S>, and black<S>7838</S> as a raven<S>6158</S>.
His eyes<S>5869</S> are like doves<S>3123</S> beside the water<S>4325</S> - brooks<S>650</S>, Washed<S>7364</S> with milk<S>2461</S>, and fitly set<S>3427</S>.

His cheeks<S>3895</S> are as a bed<S>6170</S> of spices<S>1314</S>, As banks of sweet<S>4840</S> herbs<S>4026</S>: His lips<S>8193</S> are as lilies<S>7799</S>, dropping<S>5197</S> liquid<S>5674</S> myrrh<S>4753</S>.

His hands<S>3027</S> are as rings<S>1550</S> of gold<S>2091</S> set<S>4390</S> with beryl<S>8658</S>: His body<S>4578</S> is as ivory<S>8127</S> work<S>6247</S> overlaid<S>5968</S> with sapphires<S>5601</S>.

His legs<S>7785</S> are as pillars<S>5982</S> of marble<S>8336</S>, set<S>3245</S> upon sockets<S>134</S> of fine gold<S>6337</S>: His aspect<S>4758</S> is like Lebanon<S>3844</S>, excellent<S>977</S> as the cedars<S>730</S>.

His mouth<S>2441</S> is most sweet<S>4477</S>; Yea<S>4261</S>, he is altogether lovely. This is my beloved<S>1730</S>, and this is my friend<S>7453</S>, O daughters<S>1323</S> of Jerusalem<S>3389</S>.

Whither is thy beloved<S>1730</S> gone<S>1980</S>, O thou fairest<S>3303</S> among women<S>802</S>? Whither hath thy beloved<S>1730</S> turned<S>6437</S> him<S>1245</S>, That we may seek him with thee?

My beloved<S>1730</S> is gone down<S>3381</S> to his garden<S>1588</S>, To the beds<S>6170</S> of spices<S>1314</S>, To feed<S>7462</S> in the gardens<S>1588</S>, and to gather<S>3950</S> lilies<S>7799</S>.
I am my beloved<S>1730</S>'s, and my beloved<S>1730</S> is mine<S>7462</S>; He feedeth his flock among the lilies<S>7799</S>,
Thou art fair<S>3303</S>, O my love<S>7474</S>, as Tirzah<S>8656</S>, Comely<S>5000</S> as Jerusalem<S>3389</S>, Terrible<S>366</S> as an army with banners<S>1713</S>.

Turn away<S>5437</S> thine eyes<S>5869</S> from me<S>1992</S>, For they have overcome<S>7292</S> me<S>8181</S>. Thy hair is as a flock<S>5739</S> of goats<S>5795</S>, That lie<S>1570</S> along the side of Gilead<S>1568</S>.

Thy teeth<S>8127</S> are like a flock<S>5739</S> of ewes<S>7353</S>, Which are come up<S>5927</S> from the washing<S>7367</S>; Whereof every one hath twins<S>8382</S>, And none is bereaved among them<S>7909</S>.
Thy temples<S>7541</S> are like a piece<S>6400</S> of a pomegranate<S>7416</S> Behind<S>1157</S> thy veil<S>6777</S>.
There are threescore<S>8346</S> queens<S>4436</S>, and fourscore<S>8084</S> concubines<S>6370</S>, And virgins<S>5959</S> without number<S>4557</S>.
